title: salvia japonica var. longipes
intro: Salvia japonica var. longipes, also known as Japanese sage or Japanese salvia, is a plant species that belongs to the mint family Lamiaceae and is native to East Asia. This perennial herb has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Salvia japonica var. longipes include reducing fever, relieving headaches, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular herb for treating various health conditions. Salvia japonica var. longipes can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
